howItWorks.intro: This focal length crop factor calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Equivalent Focal Length = Actual FL × Crop Factor
Equivalent Aperture (DOF) = Actual Aperture × Crop Factor
Min Shutter Speed = 1 ÷ Equivalent Focal Length
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is crop factor?
faq.answer: Crop factor is the ratio of a full-frame sensor diagonal (43.3mm) to your camera's sensor diagonal. APS-C sensors have a 1.5-1.6× crop factor, making a 50mm lens act like 75mm on full frame.

faq.question: Does crop factor affect aperture?
faq.answer: It doesn't change the actual aperture or exposure, but it does affect depth of field. A 50mm f/1.8 on APS-C gives similar DOF to a 75mm f/2.7 on full frame.

faq.question: What is the reciprocal rule?
faq.answer: For sharp handheld shots, use a shutter speed of at least 1/(equivalent focal length). A 200mm equivalent needs at least 1/200s. Image stabilization lets you go 2-5 stops slower.
